---
name: F Prime Handler Implementation Patterns
description: Common patterns for implementing port handlers, command handlers, and component callbacks
type: reference
---

# F Prime Handler Implementation Patterns

## Handler Types

### 1. Port Handlers
**Signature:** `void <portName>_handler(FwIndexType portNum, <args>)`

**Generated by:** FPP port declarations

**Must Implement:** All input port handlers

### 2. Command Handlers
**Signature:** `void <CMD_NAME>_cmdHandler(FwOpcodeType opCode, U32 cmdSeq, <args>)`

**Generated by:** FPP command declarations

**Must Implement:** All command handlers

### 3. Parameter Callbacks (Optional)
**Signature:** `void parameterUpdated(FwPrmIdType id)` or `void parametersLoaded()`

**Generated by:** FPP parameter declarations

**Override:** Optional, for post-update actions

## Port Handler Patterns

### Pattern: Echo/Passthrough
**Use Case:** Forward request to another port

**Example:** Health ping response
```cpp
void pingIn_handler(FwIndexType portNum, U32 key) {
    // Echo key back
    this->pingOut_out(0, key);
}
```

### Pattern: State Update
**Use Case:** Update component state from external input

**Example:** Rate group member
```cpp
void schedIn_handler(FwIndexType portNum, U32 context) {
    // Update state
    this->m_executionCount++;

    // Perform periodic work
    this->performPeriodicTask();

    // Emit telemetry
    this->tlmWrite_ExecutionCount(this->m_executionCount);
}
```

### Pattern: Request-Response
**Use Case:** Synchronous operation with return value

**Example:** Telemetry get
```cpp
Fw::TlmValid TlmGet_handler(FwIndexType portNum, FwChanIdType id,
                            Fw::Time& timeTag, Fw::TlmBuffer& val) {
    // Look up telemetry
    TlmEntry* entry = findEntry(id);

    if (entry && entry->valid) {
        val = entry->buffer;
        timeTag = entry->lastUpdate;
        return Fw::TlmValid::VALID;
    }

    return Fw::TlmValid::INVALID;
}
```

### Pattern: Dispatch/Route
**Use Case:** Route request to appropriate handler based on data

**Example:** Command dispatcher
```cpp
void seqCmdBuff_handler(FwIndexType portNum, Fw::ComBuffer& data, U32 context) {
    // Decode command
    Fw::CmdPacket cmdPkt;
    cmdPkt.deserializeFrom(data);

    // Look up opcode in dispatch table
    FwIndexType targetPort = lookupOpcode(cmdPkt.getOpCode());

    // Dispatch to implementing component
    if (targetPort != -1) {
        this->compCmdSend_out(targetPort, cmdPkt.getOpCode(),
                             this->m_seq++, cmdPkt.getArgBuffer());
    }
}
```

### Pattern: Collect-and-Process
**Use Case:** Accumulate data, process when complete

**Example:** Buffer accumulator
```cpp
void bufferRecv_handler(FwIndexType portNum, Fw::Buffer& buf) {
    // Append to accumulation buffer
    memcpy(&this->m_accumBuffer[this->m_accumSize],
           buf.getData(), buf.getSize());
    this->m_accumSize += buf.getSize();

    // Return buffer
    this->bufferSend_out(0, buf);

    // Check if complete
    if (this->m_accumSize >= this->m_expectedSize) {
        this->processAccumulatedData();
        this->m_accumSize = 0;
    }
}
```

## Command Handler Patterns

### Pattern: Simple Execution
**Use Case:** Command with no parameters

**Example:** No-op command
```cpp
void CMD_NO_OP_cmdHandler(FwOpcodeType opCode, U32 cmdSeq) {
    // Emit event
    this->log_ACTIVITY_HI_NoOpReceived();

    // Send success response
    this->cmdResponse_out(opCode, cmdSeq, Fw::CmdResponse::OK);
}
```

### Pattern: Validation-then-Execute
**Use Case:** Command with parameters requiring validation

**Example:** Parameter update
```cpp
void CMD_SET_THRESHOLD_cmdHandler(FwOpcodeType opCode, U32 cmdSeq, F32 threshold) {
    // Validate
    if (threshold < 0.0 || threshold > 100.0) {
        this->log_WARNING_HI_InvalidThreshold(threshold);
        this->cmdResponse_out(opCode, cmdSeq, Fw::CmdResponse::VALIDATION_ERROR);
        return;
    }

    // Execute
    this->m_threshold = threshold;

    // Emit success event
    this->log_ACTIVITY_HI_ThresholdUpdated(threshold);

    // Send success response
    this->cmdResponse_out(opCode, cmdSeq, Fw::CmdResponse::OK);
}
```

### Pattern: Asynchronous Operation
**Use Case:** Command triggers long operation

**Example:** File operation
```cpp
void CMD_LOAD_FILE_cmdHandler(FwOpcodeType opCode, U32 cmdSeq,
                               const Fw::CmdStringArg& fileName) {
    // Store command sequence for later response
    this->m_pendingCmdSeq = cmdSeq;
    this->m_pendingOpCode = opCode;

    // Initiate async operation
    Os::File::Status status = this->m_file.open(fileName.toChar(), Os::File::OPEN_READ);

    if (status != Os::File::OP_OK) {
        this->log_WARNING_HI_FileOpenError(fileName);
        this->cmdResponse_out(opCode, cmdSeq, Fw::CmdResponse::EXECUTION_ERROR);
        return;
    }

    // Start reading (response sent later when complete)
    this->m_state = READING_FILE;
}
```

### Pattern: State Transition
**Use Case:** Command changes component mode

**Example:** Mode change
```cpp
void CMD_SET_MODE_cmdHandler(FwOpcodeType opCode, U32 cmdSeq, ModeType newMode) {
    // Check if transition allowed
    if (!isValidTransition(this->m_currentMode, newMode)) {
        this->log_WARNING_HI_InvalidModeTransition(this->m_currentMode, newMode);
        this->cmdResponse_out(opCode, cmdSeq, Fw::CmdResponse::VALIDATION_ERROR);
        return;
    }

    // Perform mode-specific cleanup
    this->exitMode(this->m_currentMode);

    // Update mode
    this->m_currentMode = newMode;

    // Perform mode-specific initialization
    this->enterMode(newMode);

    // Emit event and telemetry
    this->log_ACTIVITY_HI_ModeChanged(newMode);
    this->tlmWrite_CurrentMode(newMode);

    // Send success response
    this->cmdResponse_out(opCode, cmdSeq, Fw::CmdResponse::OK);
}
```

## Parameter Callback Patterns

### Pattern: Individual Parameter Update
**Override:** `parameterUpdated(FwPrmIdType id)`

**Use Case:** Take action when specific parameter changes

**Example:**
```cpp
void parameterUpdated(FwPrmIdType id) {
    // Get parameter enum from ID
    ParameterId paramId = static_cast<ParameterId>(id);

    switch (paramId) {
        case PARAM_GAIN:
            // Recompute control matrix
            this->recomputeControlGain();
            break;

        case PARAM_THRESHOLD:
            // Reset threshold detector
            this->resetThresholdDetector();
            break;
    }
}
```

### Pattern: All Parameters Loaded
**Override:** `parametersLoaded()`

**Use Case:** Initialize after all parameters loaded

**Example:**
```cpp
void parametersLoaded() {
    // All parameters now valid
    this->m_parametersValid = true;

    // Perform initialization requiring all parameters
    this->initializeControlSystem();

    // Emit event
    this->log_ACTIVITY_HI_ParametersLoaded();
}
```

## Thread Safety Patterns

### Pattern: Guarded Port with Critical Section
```cpp
void setValue_handler(FwIndexType portNum, U32 value) {
    // Handler is guarded - mutex already locked

    // Critical section - safe to access shared state
    this->m_sharedValue = value;
    this->m_updateCount++;

    // Mutex unlocked on return
}
```

### Pattern: Async Port with Internal Lock
```cpp
void asyncUpdate_handler(FwIndexType portNum, U32 value) {
    // Handler runs on component thread, but may need lock
    // if state accessed from other paths (e.g., sync ports)

    this->lock();
    this->m_sharedState = value;
    this->unLock();
}
```

### Pattern: Double-Buffering for Lock-Free Read
```cpp
void Run_handler(FwIndexType portNum, U32 context) {
    // Swap active buffer (brief lock)
    this->lock();
    this->m_activeBuffer = 1 - this->m_activeBuffer;
    this->unLock();

    // Read from inactive buffer (no lock needed)
    this->processBuffer(this->m_buffers[1 - this->m_activeBuffer]);
}
```

## Error Handling Patterns

### Pattern: Graceful Degradation
```cpp
void handler(FwIndexType portNum, U32 value) {
    if (value > MAX_VALUE) {
        // Log warning but continue
        this->log_WARNING_LO_ValueClamped(value, MAX_VALUE);
        value = MAX_VALUE;
    }

    // Continue execution with clamped value
    this->processValue(value);
}
```

### Pattern: Fail-Fast with Assert
```cpp
void handler(FwIndexType portNum, U32 index) {
    // Critical invariant violation - assert
    FW_ASSERT(index < ARRAY_SIZE, static_cast<FwAssertArgType>(index));

    // Safe to proceed
    this->processArray(index);
}
```

### Pattern: Error Return with Event
```cpp
Fw::OpStatus handler(FwIndexType portNum, U32 value) {
    if (value == 0) {
        this->log_WARNING_HI_InvalidValue(value);
        return Fw::OpStatus::ERROR;
    }

    this->processValue(value);
    return Fw::OpStatus::OK;
}
```

## Common Anti-Patterns (Avoid These)

### Anti-Pattern: Blocking in Async Handler
**Problem:** Defeats purpose of async port
```cpp
void asyncHandler(FwIndexType portNum) {
    // BAD - Blocks component thread
    Os::Task::delay(1000);  // Don't do this!
}
```

### Anti-Pattern: Calling Back to Sender in Sync Handler
**Problem:** Can cause deadlock
```cpp
void syncHandler(FwIndexType portNum, U32 value) {
    // BAD - If caller is active component, this may deadlock
    this->notifySender_out(portNum, value);  // Dangerous!
}
```

### Anti-Pattern: Command Response in Handler (Not Command Handler)
**Problem:** Command response only valid in command handlers
```cpp
void portHandler(FwIndexType portNum) {
    // BAD - cmdResponse_out only valid in command handlers
    this->cmdResponse_out(0, 0, Fw::CmdResponse::OK);  // Wrong!
}
```

### Anti-Pattern: Heavy Computation in Guarded Handler
**Problem:** Blocks all callers with mutex held
```cpp
void guardedHandler(FwIndexType portNum) {
    // BAD - Mutex held during long computation
    for (int i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
        this->complexCalculation();  // Blocks other callers!
    }
}
```

## Best Practices

1. **Keep handlers focused:** One clear purpose per handler
2. **Validate early:** Check inputs before taking action
3. **Emit events:** Log significant actions for debugging
4. **Update telemetry:** Keep telemetry current
5. **Handle errors gracefully:** Don't let exceptions propagate
6. **Minimize critical sections:** Keep guarded handlers short
7. **Use pre-message hooks sparingly:** Only for time-critical flags
8. **Always send command responses:** Every command handler must respond
9. **Check port connections:** Use `isConnected_` before output calls
10. **Document threading assumptions:** Note which thread executes handler

**How to apply:** When implementing handlers:
1. Identify handler type (port, command, callback)
2. Choose appropriate pattern from examples above
3. Follow thread safety guidelines for port type
4. Validate inputs, handle errors, emit events
5. Keep handlers focused and efficient
